rulings.Case Title: EXPENSIFY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. TRILLER, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Triller Platform Co. And Triller Hold Co Llc As Judgment Debtors
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2026, Line 1, PETITIONER EXPENSIFY, INC.'s, Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Triller Platform Co. And Triller Hold Co LLC As Judgment Debtors. Plaintiff's motion to amend judgment to add Triller Platform Co. and Triller Hold Co LLC as additional judgment debtors is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CCH).

rulings.Case Title: YOU LIANG SONG VS. ARTHUR LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2026, Line 2, DEFENDANT ARTHUR LIU's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. (Complete tentative ruling emailed to the parties). Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. "A motion for judgment on the pleadings is akin to a general demurrer; it tests the sufficiency of the complaint to state a cause of action. [citation] The court must assume the truth of all factual allegations in the complaint, along with matters subject to judicial notice." (Wise v. Pacific Gas & Electric Co. (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 725, 738.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. (Evidence Code section 452(d) [court records].) Exhibits 1-4 are court records and exhibit 5 [6/24/21 letter] and exhibit 6 [8/17/20 letter] were attached to plaintiff's original complaint. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CCH).

rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC ET AL VS. ARI LAW, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2026, Line 9, DEFENDANT AALAEI ALI AND NILOFAR TRUST's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The Motion to Strike Complaint is granted without leave to amend, and the matter is dismissed. (Carroll v. Import Motors, Inc. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1429, 1434-1437.) The Court strikes the Complaint filed in this action on January 23, 2025. The Complaint is barred by Code of Civil Procedure section 426.30(a), which provides, "Except as otherwise provided by statute, if a party against whom a complaint has been filed and served fails to allege in a cross-complaint any related cause of action which (at the time of serving his answer to the complaint) he has against the plaintiff, such party may not thereafter in any other action assert against the plaintiff the related cause of action not pleaded." The Complaint should have been filed as a Cross-Complaint in the Santa Clara County Superior Court, in Case No. 23CV425115. The Court finds that the Complaint in this action arises from the same transaction that served as the basis for the Santa Clara County Superior Court case. (See Currie Medical Specialties, Inc. v. Bowen (1982) 136 Cal.App.3d 774, 777 ("The waiver provision of section 426.30 is mandatory, the policy in favor of hearing all related claims in a single action controlling.") For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CCH).

rulings.Case Title: STANISLAV NYU ET AL VS. BRIDGES RESTORATION LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2026, Line 13, PLAINTIFF STANISLAV NYU, JASON CAMPBELL's Motion For Protective Order; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is DENIED. The declarations of counsel LeClerc and Cravens show that neither party has met and conferred in good faith. On the one hand, defendant unilaterally set deposition dates without consultation. On the other hand, plaintiffs have not legitimately attempted to find new dates and locations for the depositions. The court orders counsel to meet and confer in person or by phone immediately to determine the new deposition dates, times, and locations. The court will not limit the Campbell deposition to three hours. The depositions shall occur by March 9, 2026. Counsel Le Clerc and Cravens shall each remit $500 to the court by March 9, 2026, because they both acted without substantial justification and this dispute should have been resolved without court intervention. (CCP 2023.010, 2023.020, 2023.030.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
